title: thelocactus conothelos subsp. flavus
intro: Thelocactus conothelos subsp. flavus, a subspecies of the Thelocactus conothelos cactus, is a small, globular plant native to Mexico, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and distinctive growth patterns. The medicinal properties of Thelocactus conothelos subsp. flavus include its potential to stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion, as well as its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of Thelocactus conothelos subsp. flavus can be made by drying the plant's flowers, stems, or roots, and then infusing them in hot water to create a tea or using them in traditional folk medicine.
